messages.content:
Dollo and his colleagues studied the conservation of the ____________using a traditional knowledge system from Arunachal Pradesh, India
a.
Javan Rhinos
b.
Amur Leopard
c.
The Indian Auroch
d.
Dremomys lokriah
d. Dremomys lokriah

The Department of Research and Technology (DST) was established in 1971 to encourage new areas of?
a.
Science and Industry
b.
Science and Technology
c.
Science and Engineering
d.
Science and Development
b. Science and Technology

Bhakti movement took place in?
a.
Tamil Nadu
b.
Kerala
c.
Karnataka
d.
Delhi
a. Tamil Nadu

What is the dance of Mizoram?
a.
Garga
b.
Koli
c.
Bhangra
d.
Bambo
d. Bambo

Indian culture is a synthesis of many different human cultural trends that promotes __________ and ______________.
a.
Equality and Justice
b.
Equality and Sovereignty
c.
Organic and Harmonious
d.
Tradition and knowledge
c. Organic and Harmonious

Individual ethnic and tribal communities, in different regions of the world, have preserved _________________
a.
Knowledge provides context and explanations for artifacts, site modifications, and evidence.
b.
Different versions of traditional knowledge.
c.
Ritual activities, in public and private venues.
d.
Knowledge provides a crucial framework that situates and explains these people from the past.
b. Different versions of traditional knowledge.

Traditional knowledge learned through ______________
a.
Didactic
b.
Video
c.
Storytelling
d.
Audio or Music
c. Storytelling

The ethnic and indigenous people are known to maintain a large diversity of plants, including agricultural plants, wild fruits, seeds, bulbs, roots, and tubers since they must rely on these sources for food purposes is called as _______________.
a.
Conserving Natural resources
b.
Conservation of Natural Flora
c.
Conservation of Natural Water
d.
Conservation of Natural Faun
b. Conservation of Natural Flora

From __________ and ___________ Indians practice a variety of cultures, religions, languages, and traditions in the country.
a.
Jammu and Kashmir to Kanyakumari
b.
Ladakh and Kanyakumari
c.
Srinagar and Kanyakumari
d.
Kanyakumari and Jammu and Kashmir
a. Jammu and Kashmir to Kanyakumari

Who emphasised the benefits of religion for society regardless of how it is practised or which particular religious beliefs a society prefers
a.
Emile Durkheim
b.
Max Webber
c.
Augus Comte
d.
Karl Marx
a. Emile Durkheim

What is Haryana's folk song?
a.
Ragini
b.
Bhatiali
c.
Mand
d.
Gulraj
c. Mand

Which is not the characteristics of Culture?
a.
Culture is a Symbol
b.
Culture is Transmittable
c.
Culture is dynamic
d.
Culture is Social
b. Culture is Transmittable

Which disease was eradicated by Medical and Health Sciences?
a.
Chickenpox
b.
Measles
c.
Smallpox
d.
Paramyxovirus
c. Smallpox

The Sanskrit words patta meaning ________ and Chitra meaning ___________.
a.
Canvas and picture
b.
Sensational and impressive
c.
Creative and design
d.
Colourful and beauties
a. Canvas and picture

Ramayana's original script was written by ____________.
a.
Maharishi Valmiki
b.
Kamban
c.
Bal Gangadhar Tilak
d.
Krishna
a. Maharishi Valmiki

According to ___________ the publishing of books and newspapers by the missionaries and the spread of English-language schooling undoubtedly reduced the influence of the classics and facilitated the emergence and development of regional languages.
a.
Vedanayagam Pillai
b.
Jawaharla Nehru
c.
G.U.Pope
d.
Bishop Robert Caldwell
d. Bishop Robert Caldwell

The name Pithora also refers to the Hindu deity of marriage and functions commonly popular in states of ____________ and ____________.
a.
Gujarat and Orissa
b.
Bihar and Rajasthan
c.
Kerala and Andhra Pradesh
d.
Madhya Pradesh and Gujarat
d. Madhya Pradesh and Gujarat

Islam is the world's second most populous religion, with over_________of the world's population identifying as Muslim.
a.
21%
b.
23%
c.
15%
d.
19%

How many rhythmic cycles are there in Carnatic music?
a.
3
b.
4
c.
5
d.
7

The indigenous people are the true inhabitants of the particular geographical location who have ____________ and ______________.
a.
Cultures and beliefs
b.
Customs and beliefs
c.
Knowledge and cultures
d.
Knowledge and customs
c. Knowledge and cultures

Ramayana glorifies the ________________.
a.
Krishna incarnation
b.
Agastya
c.
Rama incarnation
d.
Harivamsa
c. Rama incarnation

___________and______________ painting took Indian painting to new heights by adding new dimensions to it.
a.
Warli painting and Kalighat painting
b.
Kerala Murals and Pichwai painting
c.
Miniature Painting and Patachitra painting
d.
Gond Painting and Phad Painting.
c. Miniature Painting and Patachitra painting

The orgin of yoga is found in?
a.
Vaisheshika
b.
Yogasutra of Pantajali
c.
Nyaya of Pantajali
d.
Sutra of Pantajali
b. Yogasutra of Pantajali

Traditional knowledge of Kani tribals in Kouthalai of Tirunelveli hills, Tamil Nadu, India studied by
a.
Manhas Kewal Kumar & Rani Magotra
b.
M.Ayyanar and S.Ignacimuthu
c.
Mihin Dollo and G.V Gopi
d.
Mangala Hirwade and Anil Hirwade
b. M. Ayyanar and S. Ignacimuthu

Hypoglycemia can be treated using ____________.
a.
Jackfruit seed chutney
b.
Banana stem juice
c.
Latex papaya tree
d.
Mango peel chutney
a. Jackfruit seed chutney

according to Hinduism Sanskrit is the oldest language which is used by __________________________ for communication and dialogue and also by Indo-Aryans.
a.
Hindu celestial god
b.
Sikhist god
c.
Buddhist god
d.
Jainism god
a. Hindu celestial god

Who worte the commentaries on the Upanishads, Brahmasutras and the Bhagavad Gita?
a.
Shankaracharya
b.
Valmiki
c.
Kambar
d.
Brihaspati
a. Shankaracharya

Who are the saints played an important role in bringing the Muslims and Hindus together?
a.
St.Francis and St.John
b.
Sai Baba and Madhava
c.
Ramanuja and Sankara
d.
Sufi and Bhakti
d. Sufi and Bhakti

According to M.Ayyanar and S.Ignacimuthu study  ___________ have the potential to form the basis of pharmaceutical drugs for the treatment of a range of diseases.
a.
Traditional medicines
b.
Solanaceae family
c.
Entomophagy
d.
Biopiracy
a. Traditional medicines

A set of rules and standards in society that keep people bound to traditional standards and the use of formalised mechanisms is called?
a.
Social Structure
b.
Social Engineering
c.
Social Order
d.
Social Control
d. Social Control

What is the meaning of "Punartal" in the fivefold physiographical divisions?
a.
Separation
b.
Bewailing
c.
Union
d.
Sulking
c. Union

Raja Ravi Varma achieved recognition for his paintings from the epics of the__________ and _________________.
a.
Raghuvamsa and Budhacharita,
b.
Silappadikaram and Kumarasambava
c.
Mahabharata and Ramayana
d.
Shishupala Vadha and Kiratarjuniya
c. Mahabharata and Ramayana

Varsha ritu means____________.
a.
Monsoon
b.
Winter
c.
Autumn
d.
Spring
a. Monsoon

Folk Art is a body of art forms that originated and developed in __________________.
a.
Australia
b.
India
c.
South Africa
d.
Afghanistan
b. India

Who is the founder of Charvaka School of philosophy?
a.
Brihaspati
b.
Kambar
c.
Valmiki
d.
Shankaracharya
a. Brihaspati

Sanskrit is the only language that crossed _______________ and ______________ boundaries.
a.
Spirituality and divine
b.
Geographical and cultural
c.
individual and communal
d.
Tradition and culture
b. Geographical and cultural

Sanskrit comes from the combination of "Sam" and "Krit" which means.
a.
Complete and control
b.
Completely and done
c.
Nature and creator
d.
Creator and creation
d. Creator and creation

How many years did Gautama Buddha meditated in forests?
a.
8 Years
b.
4 Years
c.
6 Years
d.
9 Year
c. 6 Years

In the 16th century, the Mughal emperor ____________ built an atelier for the artists to promote the rich art form.
a.
Akbar
b.
Jahangir
c.
Shah Jahan
d.
Babur
a. Akbar

_____________ is important for a country to function democratically.
a.
Communism
b.
monopolism
c.
Socialism
d.
Secularism
d. Secularism

The traditional knowledge is passed from one generation to another generation which is _____________ and ______________.
a.
Holistic nature and credibility
b.
Religion and culture
c.
Cultures and beliefs
d.
Societal and economical
a. Holistic nature and credibility

Studies on Traditional Knowledge of Ethnomedicinal Plants in Jawalamukhi, Himachal Pradesh, India was conducted by
a.
Mangala Hirwade
b.
S.K Panda and his colleagues
c.
Sharma Arti and her colleagues
d.
Mihin Dollo and his colleagues
c. Sharma Arti and her colleagues

According to Bhatiaa and his colleagues, a total of _____toxic plants identified from the Udhampur district, India.
a.
60
b.
70
c.
50
d.
90
